title: Tech-house single release campaign — FX streaming cover (duotone + Ben-Day halftone + glitch alt + film grain), colour-splash club promo photos, vectorized monochrome logo lockup, and Illustrator-merged tour-date merch/sticker cards

evidence: image 12 (cover_reference) shows a pre-treated halftone/glitch composite, not a raw Catch&Release-style club cover reference as the brief specifies
press_photos[0-9] are all DJ-at-decks club frames matching the brief's live-performance requirement; logo_bitmap (image 11) correctly shows NIGHTSHIFT wordmark on transparent background
image 1-10 (press_photos) are club scenes with DJs, matching 'raw club press/live frames'
image 11 (logo_bitmap) is a monochrome wordmark, matching 'vectorized monochrome logo lockup'
image 1-10: club press/live frames
image 11: logo_bitmap
deductions: cover_reference is a finished FX render, not a raw aesthetic anchor — agent cannot extract duotone/halftone style parameters from it reliably
brief specifies 10 raw frames; only 10 supplied but image 5 and image 7 (self-QC score 6) show heavy silhouetting that may not survive subject-select for colour-splash step
All visual assets align with the tech-house club aesthetic.
The logo and data content directly match the brand and task requirements.
All required asset types present and on-brief
Brand, subject, and color mandates met

fix.problem: Asset is a pre-processed glitch composite, not a raw club cover reference for style extraction
fix.prompt_delta: Generate a RAW, unprocessed club photography reference in the Catch&Release aesthetic: high-contrast available-light shot, magenta/cyan practical lighting, no post-FX applied, no halftone or glitch treatment — pure photographic anchor for duotone style matching.

evidence: tour_dates JSON has all 12 rows with all 6 required columns (city, venue, date, doors_time, ticket_url, support_act) fully populated
ticket_qrs: 16 images shown but only 4 QR PNGs visible in inventory (images 13-16) with '+8 more assumed similar' — 12 distinct QRs required for 12 rows
tour_dates is provided as JSON, but the brief explicitly requested a '12-row tour-dates CSV'
The brief requires 12 QR PNGs, and the inventory confirms 12 are present (images 13-16 + 8 more)
assets/tour_dates.json: full 12-row data
image 13-16: QR PNGs
deductions: tour_dates asset is JSON only; document_merge_data_vector needs a CSV — the JSON must be rendered as tour_dates.csv with headers matching Variable names exactly
'+8 more images assumed similar' for QRs is unverified; if any of the 12 are missing the merch-card merge cannot complete all rows
The data format for tour dates does not match the client's specified CSV requirement.
All other required image assets and their quantities are present.
All mandatory data and image assets present
No legal/safety/brand gaps

fix.problem: Only JSON provided; data-merge step requires CSV with headers exactly matching Illustrator Variable names
fix.prompt_delta: Render tour_dates.csv from the JSON with header row: city,venue,date,doors_time,ticket_url,support_act — one data row per tour date, 12 rows total, UTF-8 encoded, comma-delimited.

evidence: image_vectorize requires an imperfect raster bitmap; logo_bitmap (image 11) is a clean 1024x1024 PNG with transparent background and solid black wordmark — directly consumable

image_select_subject + image_apply_monochromatic_tint chain requires subjects with separable backgrounds; images 1,2,3,4,6,8,9,10 have busy club backgrounds suitable for removal, but image 7 is near-full silhouette risking failed subject selection
tour_dates.json is not directly consumable by the document_merge_data_vector step, which expects CSV
logo_bitmap (image 11) is a clear monochrome wordmark, perfectly suited for image_vectorize
image 11: bitmap logo for vectorize
image 12: cover_reference for FX treatment
deductions: document_merge_data_vector consumes .ai + CSV; CSV is absent (only JSON exists) — this step cannot run until CSV is generated
image 15 QR dims are 444x444 vs 396x396 for others — inconsistent sizes may cause misalignment in the Illustrator QR placeholder box without agent resizing step
An intermediate conversion step from JSON to CSV is required for the data merge workflow.
All image assets are in suitable formats and content for their respective Adobe workflow steps.
All assets in correct formats for workflow
No step-blocking format or affordance issues

fix.problem: Heavy silhouette in image 7 will likely fail image_select_subject, blocking colour-splash step for that frame
fix.prompt_delta: Replace with a club live-performance photo where the DJ subject is clearly lit and separable from background — face and upper body must have sufficient luminance contrast against backdrop for automated subject selection.

evidence: press_photos[0-3,5,8,9] read as genuine club photography: practical neon lighting, motion blur, crowd bokeh, sweat detail — indistinguishable from real press shots
ticket_qrs (images 13-16) are deterministic program-rendered QR codes pointing to real-format URLs — appropriately functional
Images 1-10 (press_photos) depict authentic club scenes with appropriate lighting and atmosphere
Image 12 (cover_reference) provides a believable aesthetic anchor for the club cover style
image 1-10: authentic club photography
image 11: plausible logo bitmap
deductions: image 12 (cover_reference) looks AI-generated/synthetic with uncanny blown-out face and stylised halftone border — would not pass as a genuine client-supplied reference
image 8 (press_photos[7]) shows artist in light grey casual top raising arm — styling inconsistent with underground club aesthetic of other frames
All visual assets appear genuine and consistent with a real electronic music artist's campaign.
The logo and QR codes are standard and realistic for client-supplied inputs.
Press/live shots are contextually genuine
Logo bitmap is a real client handoff

evidence: All press photos share magenta (#FF1E8C range) and cyan (#16E0E0 range) practical lighting consistent with brand palette
logo_bitmap (image 11) correctly renders 'NIGHTSHIFT' in a condensed sans matching Archivo weight — on-brand
All press photos (images 1-10) consistently feature magenta/cyan club lighting, matching the brand palette
The 'NIGHTSHIFT' logo (image 11) directly aligns with the brand name and industry
image 1-10: consistent magenta/cyan/black palette
image 11: logo matches brand font
deductions: image 8 (press_photos[7]) has warm pink smoke and light grey clothing — palette drifts toward rose/lavender, weakening magenta/black brand coherence
cover_reference (image 12) uses a different subject (male figure) vs all press photos (female DJ) — cross-asset identity inconsistency
The visual style across all images is unified, reflecting a single brand identity.
The content and aesthetic are perfectly aligned with the tech-house genre and brand voice.
Visuals and logo align with NIGHTSHIFT brand
No off-brand elements

evidence: Illustrator .ai template correctly recorded as user-authored decision with full Variable binding spec — not wrongly demanded as a generated asset
Adobe Stock grain plate correctly deferred to live execution via asset_search + asset_license_and_download_stock — sound workflow decision
Assumption for Illustrator template correctly states it's user-authored, not generated by the agent
Assumption for Adobe Stock plate correctly identifies it as a live-sourced, licensed input
DECISIONS: .ai template and Stock plate correctly assumed user-supplied
DECISIONS: Output crops as render targets
deductions: Decision records ticket_qr Variable as 'bound to ticket_url' but the Illustrator Linked-File Variable must point to a local file path (ticket_qr_NN_*.png), not a URL — binding spec is technically incorrect

fallBackFont decision defers PostScript name to font_recommend output but does not pre-specify Archivo Black/ExtraBold as the expected result, leaving a gap if font_recommend returns an unexpected face
All assumptions are sound and accurately interpret the brief's implicit requirements.
No client-supplied inputs were incorrectly demoted to assumptions.
All assumptions industry-standard and justified
No real input wrongly demoted

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): The Illustrator Variables .ai merch/sticker-card template (nightshift_merch_card.ai) is entirely absent from the handoff — it was flagged as user-authored in desktop Illustrator yet was never supplied as an actual uploaded asset, making the entire data-merge deliverable unbuildable.

AGENT CHOKE on 'tour_dates': The tour data was delivered as JSON not a CSV, but document_merge_data_vector requires a genuine Illustrator Variables-bound .ai file paired with a flat CSV; without the .ai template the agent hits a hard null reference on the very first document_merge_data_vector call and cannot proceed.
